reasoning: The NFCI and its subindexes are heavily smoothed via a dynamic factor model and Kalman filtering, leading to high serial correlation and distinct multi-month cycles. The risk subindex recently troughed at -0.512 on Jan 31, 2025, and has exhibited a steady, accelerating 4-week uptrend to -0.485 for the week ending Feb 28, 2025. Given this strong, smoothed momentum, the 1-week (March 9) and 1-month (April 1) horizons are highly likely to realize continued increases. Prior cycles have typically lasted around 5-6 months without interruption (e.g. a steady March to Aug 2024 uptrend, followed by an Aug 2024 to Jan 2025 downtrend). This cyclical behavior suggests the index will likely remain above -0.485 through the 3-month and 6-month horizons, though confidence decays over time as the index may peak and turn down. For the 12-month horizon, timing in the cycle is very uncertain, but the current low reference value of -0.485 indicates relatively loose conditions compared to the long-term historical mean of 0. This creates an asymmetric upside risk (reversion to the mean), supporting a probability somewhat above 0.50.
